rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. SLOAN SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation; Memo P/A

Granted per the stipulation of the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE HOVEY VS. KATHLEEN WELSH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Relief
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 6, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF KATHERINE HOVEY'S Motion For Relief. Denied. Since the motion was filed more than six months after plaintiff filed a dismissal and the entry of judgment, it is untimely per Code of Civil Procedure 473(b). Even if plaintiff's December 12 ex parte application did serve to toll the six month limitation period in section 473(b), plaintiff does not provide any grounds why the judgment, as distinct from the dismissal, should be set aside. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH JANE LARSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 6, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. The City satisfied its initial burden showing that there were legitimate, non-discriminatory reasons for its actions, it did not fail to engage in the interactive process or accommodate plaintiff's disability, and any harassment was neither severe nor pervasive. Plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue of pretext or otherwise showing a triable issue as to any of her remaining eight claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH JANE LARSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 6, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF SARAH LARSON'S Motion To File Second Amended Complaint. Denied without prejudice to filing a new lawsuit. The ruling on the City's motion for summary judgment makes it advisable for plaintiff to start anew rather than prolonging this case that has now been adjudicated in its entirety adversely to her. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WALTER ZOLEZZI VS. THE ROYAL TOWERS APARTMENT CORPORATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 6, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT ROYAL TOWERS APARTMENTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. There is a triable issue whether concealed pipes or other plumbing fixtures that were not susceptible to plaintiff's inspection caused the harm and thus whether the "exculpatory clause" applies to this case. The business judgment rule and Lamden deference are inapplicable. The harm was the result of nonfeasance, not a business decision. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGUS M. CAMERON VS. SHARON L. HAMILTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn Compelling Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 6, 2012, Line 21, PLAINTIFF ANGUS CAMERON'S Motion Compelling Production Of Documents. The court adopts in full the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley as follows: DENIED. Plaintiff's November 15, 2011 subpoena violates the one deposition limit for natural persons does not apply because plaintiff's first subpoena was not necessary as preliminary discovery to ascertain the existence of documents or categories of documents after which a second deposition concerning the documents would be permissible. Requests for sanctions by both parties are denied. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL EHRLICH VS. HAROLD ZLOT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Subpoenas To Thomas Weisel Partners Group, Inc., Merrill Lynch Commodities, Inc. And Foldman Sachs Specialty Lending Holdings, Inc. Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedures
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 6, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL EHRLICH Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Subpoenas To Thomas Weisel Partners Group, Inc., Merrill Lynch Commodities, Inc. And Foldman Sachs Specialty Lending Holdings, Inc. Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedures Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to quash is granted. Subpoenas are overly broad an insufficient showing of relevance to overcome the right to privacy as provided under California law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al VS. JABER A ALGAHIM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 6, 2012, Line 20, Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. Granted in part, denied in part, and hearing required in part. All requested injunctive relief is granted except the request to prohibit sending certain money out of state and provision of private security and surveillance equipment. The requested relief prohibiting sending certain money out of state is denied. A hearing is required to discuss the propriety of granting the requested relief regarding private security and surveillance equipment. Plaintiffs have shown that they are likely to prevail on the merits of their Drug Abatement Act claim and that both the public interest and the balance of hardships favors granting appropriately tailored preliminary injunctive relief. All of defendant's arguments why injunctive relief should not be granted - including lack of evidence of defendant's violation of the Drug Abatement Act and irreparable harm, discriminatory "prosecution," laches and unclean hands - lack merit. Defendant does not offer any argument against the scope of the requested injunctive relief other than the request to prohibit sending certain money out of state and provision of private security and surveillance equipment. Plaintiffs have not shown the need to prohibit sending money out of state is not supported by the evidence. The court wishes to hear further from the parties to evaluate the propriety of granting the requested relief regarding private security and surveillance equipment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
